worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Greg Brockman's own framing of Workspace Agents — Codex-hosted, Slack-connected, recurring tasks — is the primary source on the biggest launch of the day.

worthClicking.author: @levie
worthClicking.why: Aaron Levie's 882K-view take argues enterprise agent adoption is gated by legacy stacks, not model capability — directly relevant to sales and GTM positioning.

worthClicking.author: @ClaudeDevs
worthClicking.why: Anthropic's official blog on MCP in production (direct API vs CLI vs MCP decision framework) is the most substantive technical post in the feed.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Crisp thesis: OpenAI Workspace Agents shortened every SaaS company's AI roadmap by 18 months — useful competitive framing for product strategy.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Claude Code wiping out $25B in vertical AI market cap is a provocative but grounded competitive map worth stress-testing.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Karpathy's 1B-param / clean-data = frontier-quality claim gets the clearest public explanation here, with real implications for model cost and build-vs-buy decisions.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concrete math on why UGC ad creative costs ($15K for 50 variants) are the real distribution bottleneck for solo vibe-coded apps — directly actionable for indie devs.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Brex open-sourcing their OpenClaw operating model is a rare look at how a scaled company is actually structuring agent-native operations.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: ChatGPT plugin for Google Sheets is a quiet but high-surface-area distribution move that puts AI directly into the spreadsheet workflows of every non-technical user.

voices.why: Most substantive PM-strategy voice in the feed today — multiple original theses on agent-native org structure, Claude Code disruption, and indie dev distribution math.
Consistently sharp on enterprise adoption friction; his takes have reach and are grounded in actual sales/deployment realities.
Primary source for Anthropic tooling and agent architecture guidance — no noise, all signal for builders.
Catches things others miss (embedding model deprecation risks, GPT-imagegen-2 prompt rewriting) with calm, practical analysis.

examples: RoundtableSpace all-caps hype recaps of already-covered launches
off-topic content (creatine dosing, street trees, A24 box office, MJ estate)
